{{ill|lt=Great Purification|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} Rite of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nagoshi|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=July 7|Q2694|WD=Q2694|1=July 7}} — Five Seasonal Festivals ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanabata Festival|1=UNKNOWN}}) * First Saturday of August — {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Grand Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} Eve Festival, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nashi Fireworks|1=UNKNOWN}} Safety Prayer, {{ill|lt=Parishioners|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} Officers’ Report Ceremony, Parishioner Safety Prayer * First Sunday of August — Grand Festival Main Ceremony, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hama-yumi|1=UNKNOWN}} Ritual, {{ill|lt=Devotees|Q1579384|WD=Q1579384|1=Veneration}} Report Ceremony, Devotee Safety Prayer * {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856|1=September 9}} — Five Seasonal Festivals ({{ill|lt=Chrysanthemum Festival|Q16909838|WD=Q16909838|1=Chrysanthemum Day}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōyō no Sekku|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Autumnal Equinox Day — Autumn Joint Ancestral Festival (Soreisha) * {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}} — {{ill|lt=Niinamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} * {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}} — {{ill|lt=Great Purification|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} Rite, {{ill|lt=New Year’s Eve|Q7245702|WD=Q7245702|1=Chinese New Year's Eve}} Festival ; Monthly * 1st — Monthly Festival * 15th — Monthly Festival ; Daily * Daily Offering Rite === Other === * One of the shrine’s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gonnegi|1=UNKNOWN}} (assistant priests), who is also a motorcyclist, began providing support services for riders. This led to the shrine becoming known as a “Motorcycle Shrine.”[https://hamamatsu-lab.jp/goout/2327.html Published: 2021/10/04 “Ōtoshi Shrine, the Motorcycle Shrine of Hamamatsu, the Home of Motorcycles” Hamalabo – Hamamatsu Research Institute][https://forride.jp/motorcycle/sizuoka-touring Published: 2020/08/23 Updated: 2020/08/23 “A Local Motorcycle Girl’s Guide! Shizuoka Holiday Touring Spots” PLAY For Ride] === References === * “Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals Nationwide,” Association of Shinto Shrines, 1995 === Citations === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.ootoshi-jinja.or.jp/ Engishiki Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine] {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tennōgū Ōtoshi Jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の建築物]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:オートバイ]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Torii gate and worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|44|14.2|N|137|46|15.9|E}}}} |Location= {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}} 1484-1 Tennō-chō |Coordinates= {{coord|34|44|14.2|N|137|46|15.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities= {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} {{ill|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} (associated) {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}} {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta Taneko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Emperor Chōkei|Q464039|WD=Q464039|1=Emperor Chōkei}} {{ill|lt=Emperor Kameyama|Q360972|WD=Q360972|1=Emperor Kameyama}} {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Uda|Q440538|WD=Q440538|1=Emperor Go-Uda}} {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nijō|Q374075|WD=Q374075|1=Emperor Go-Nijō}} {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}} {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Murakami|Q440447|WD=Q440447|1=Emperor Go-Murakami}} |Shrine rank= Former village shrine; Shikinaisha |Established= Unknown |Annual festival= Nearest Sunday to {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} Grand Festival |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ōtoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine''' (てんのうぐうおおとしじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Tennō-chō, {{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. One of its component shrines, Ōtoshi Shrine, is listed among the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province|Q11642130|WD=Q11642130|lt=Engishiki shrines|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}}. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. Originally, the shrine consisted of three separate shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tennōgū|1=UNKNOWN}}, Tennō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|Q11436758|WD=Q11436758|1=Ōtoshi Shrines}}—which were later merged. Subsequently, the tutelary deities of four parish towns were also enshrined together, resulting in the present form. The collective name Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine came into use thereafter. === Enshrined deities === ''' Tennōgū ''' * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta Taneko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ''' Tennō Shrine ''' * {{ill|lt=Emperor Chōkei|Q464039|WD=Q464039|1=Emperor Chōkei}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Kameyama|Q360972|WD=Q360972|1=Emperor Kameyama}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Uda|Q440538|WD=Q440538|1=Emperor Go-Uda}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nijō|Q374075|WD=Q374075|1=Emperor Go-Nijō}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Murakami|Q440447|WD=Q440447|1=Emperor Go-Murakami}} ''' Ōtoshi Shrine ''' * Ōtoshi-no-Kami === Subsidiary shrines === * Mikuwainari Shrine * Soreisha (Ancestral Spirit Shrine) === Events === * {{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}} — New Year’s Day Festival, Burnt Offerings Festival, {{ill|lt=Genshisai|Q11387821|WD=Q11387821|1=Genshi-sai}} * {{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228|1=January 7}} — {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Five Seasonal Festivals|1=UNKNOWN}} (Seven Herbs Festival / Wakakusa no Sekku) * {{ill|lt=January 28|Q2285|WD=Q2285|1=January 28}} — Burnt Offerings Festival * {{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|WD=Q2315|1=February 3}} — {{ill|lt=Setsubun|Q746798|WD=Q746798|1=Setsubun}} Festival * First Day of the Horse — {{ill|lt=Hatsuuma|Q510847|WD=Q510847|1=Hatsuuma}} Festival * {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}} — {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} / {{ill|lt=National Foundation Day|Q11604982|WD=Q11604982|1=Kigensetsu}} Festival * {{ill|lt=March 3|Q2391|WD=Q2391|1=March 3}} — Five Seasonal Festivals ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Peach Festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Jōshi no Sekku|1=UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141|WD=Q1049141|1=Vernal Equinox Day}} — Spring Joint Ancestral Festival (Soreisha) * First Day of the Horse — Mikuwainari Shrine Annual Festival * {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} — Five Seasonal Festivals ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iris Festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tango no Sekku|1=UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill|lt=May 15|Q2562|WD=Q2562|1=May 15}} — {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tea Offering|1=UNKNOWN}} Ceremony * {{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}} — {{ill|lt=Great Purification|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} Rite of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nagoshi|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=July 7|Q2694|WD=Q2694|1=July 7}} — Five Seasonal Festivals ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanabata Festival|1=UNKNOWN}}) * First Saturday of August — {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Grand Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} Eve Festival, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nashi Fireworks|1=UNKNOWN}} Safety Prayer, {{ill|lt=Parishioners|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} Officers’ Report Ceremony, Parishioner Safety Prayer * First Sunday of August — Grand Festival Main Ceremony, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hama-yumi|1=UNKNOWN}} Ritual, {{ill|lt=Devotees|Q1579384|WD=Q1579384|1=Veneration}} Report Ceremony, Devotee Safety Prayer * {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856|1=September 9}} — Five Seasonal Festivals ({{ill|lt=Chrysanthemum Festival|Q16909838|WD=Q16909838|1=Chrysanthemum Day}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōyō no Sekku|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Autumnal Equinox Day — Autumn Joint Ancestral Festival (Soreisha) * {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}} — {{ill|lt=Niinamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} * {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}} — {{ill|lt=Great Purification|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} Rite, {{ill|lt=New Year’s Eve|Q7245702|WD=Q7245702|1=Chinese New Year's Eve}} Festival ; Monthly * 1st — Monthly Festival * 15th — Monthly Festival ; Daily * Daily Offering Rite === Other === * One of the shrine’s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gonnegi|1=UNKNOWN}} (assistant priests), who is also a motorcyclist, began providing support services for riders. This led to the shrine becoming known as a “Motorcycle Shrine.”[https://hamamatsu-lab.jp/goout/2327.html Published: 2021/10/04 “Ōtoshi Shrine, the Motorcycle Shrine of Hamamatsu, the Home of Motorcycles” Hamalabo – Hamamatsu Research Institute][https://forride.jp/motorcycle/sizuoka-touring Published: 2020/08/23 Updated: 2020/08/23 “A Local Motorcycle Girl’s Guide! Shizuoka Holiday Touring Spots” PLAY For Ride] === References === * “Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals Nationwide,” Association of Shinto Shrines, 1995 === Citations === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.ootoshi-jinja.or.jp/ Engishiki Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine] {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の建築物]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:オートバイ]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Shikinaisha |Established= Unknown |Annual festival= Nearest Sunday to {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} Grand Festival |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ōtoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine''' (てんのうぐうおおとしじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Tennō-chō, {{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. One of its component shrines, Ōtoshi Shrine, is listed among the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province|Q11642130|WD=Q11642130|lt=Engishiki shrines|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}}. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. Originally, the shrine consisted of three separate shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tennōgū|1=UNKNOWN}}, Tennō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|Q11436758|WD=Q11436758|1=Ōtoshi Shrines}}—which were later merged. Subsequently, the tutelary deities of four parish towns were also enshrined together, resulting in the present form. The collective name Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine came into use thereafter. === Enshrined deities === ''' Tennōgū ''' * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}, Shikinaisha |Established= Unknown |Annual festival= Nearest Sunday to {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} Grand Festival |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ōtoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine''' (てんのうぐうおおとしじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Tennō-chō, {{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. One of its component shrines, Ōtoshi Shrine, is listed among the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province|Q11642130|WD=Q11642130|lt=Engishiki shrines|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}}. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. Originally, the shrine consisted of three separate shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tennōgū|1=UNKNOWN}}, Tennō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|Q11436758|WD=Q11436758|1=Ōtoshi Shrines}}—which were later merged. Subsequently, the tutelary deities of four parish towns were also enshrined together, resulting in the present form. The collective name Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine came into use thereafter. === Enshrined deities === ''' Tennōgū ''' * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, Shikinaisha |Established= Unknown |Annual festival= Nearest Sunday to {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} Grand Festival |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ōtoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine''' (てんのうぐうおおとしじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Tennō-chō, {{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. One of its component shrines, Ōtoshi Shrine, is listed among the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province|Q11642130|WD=Q11642130|lt=Engishiki shrines|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}}. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. Originally, the shrine consisted of three separate shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tennōgū|1=UNKNOWN}}, Tennō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|Q11436758|WD=Q11436758|1=Ōtoshi Shrines}}—which were later merged. Subsequently, the tutelary deities of four parish towns were also enshrined together, resulting in the present form. The collective name Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine came into use thereafter. === Enshrined deities === ''' Tennōgū ''' * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, Shikinaisha |Established= Unknown |Annual festival= Nearest Sunday to {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} Grand Festival |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ōtoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine''' (てんのうぐうおおとしじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Tennō-chō, {{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. One of its component shrines, Ōtoshi Shrine, is listed among the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province|Q11642130|WD=Q11642130|lt=Engishiki shrines|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}}. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. Originally, the shrine consisted of three separate shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tennōgū|1=UNKNOWN}}, Tennō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|Q11436758|WD=Q11436758|1=Ōtoshi Shrines}}—which were later merged. Subsequently, the tutelary deities of four parish towns were also enshrined together, resulting in the present form. The collective name Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine came into use thereafter. === Enshrined deities === ''' Tennōgū ''' * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, Shikinaisha |Established= Unknown |Annual festival= Nearest Sunday to {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} Grand Festival |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ōtoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine''' (てんのうぐうおおとしじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Tennō-chō, {{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. One of its component shrines, Ōtoshi Shrine, is listed among the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province|Q11642130|WD=Q11642130|lt=Engishiki shrines|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}}. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. Originally, the shrine consisted of three separate shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tennōgū|1=UNKNOWN}}, Tennō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|Q11436758|WD=Q11436758|1=Ōtoshi Shrines}}—which were later merged. Subsequently, the tutelary deities of four parish towns were also enshrined together, resulting in the present form. The collective name Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine came into use thereafter. === Enshrined deities === ''' Tennōgū ''' * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta Taneko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ''' Tennō Shrine ''' * {{ill|lt=Emperor Chōkei|Q464039|WD=Q464039|1=Emperor Chōkei}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Kameyama|Q360972|WD=Q360972|1=Emperor Kameyama}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Uda|Q440538|WD=Q440538|1=Emperor Go-Uda}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nijō|Q374075|WD=Q374075|1=Emperor Go-Nijō}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Murakami|Q440447|WD=Q440447|1=Emperor Go-Murakami}} ''' Ōtoshi Shrine ''' * Ōtoshi-no-Kami === Subsidiary shrines === * Mikuwainari Shrine * Soreisha (Ancestral Spirit Shrine) === Events === * {{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}} — New Year’s Day Festival, Burnt Offerings Festival, {{ill|lt=Genshisai|Q11387821|WD=Q11387821|1=Genshi-sai}} * {{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228|1=January 7}} — {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Five Seasonal Festivals|1=UNKNOWN}} (Seven Herbs Festival / Wakakusa no Sekku) * {{ill|lt=January 28|Q2285|WD=Q2285|1=January 28}} — Burnt Offerings Festival * {{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|WD=Q2315|1=February 3}} — {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} / {{ill|lt=National Foundation Day|Q11604982|WD=Q11604982|1=Kigensetsu}} Festival * {{ill|lt=March 3|Q2391|WD=Q2391|1=March 3}} — Five Seasonal Festival (Soreisha) * First Day of the Horse — Mikuwainari Shrine Annual Festival * {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} — Five Seasonal Festivals ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iris Festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Great Purification|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} Rite of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nagoshi|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=July 7|Q2694|WD=Q2694|1=July 7}} — Five Seasonal Festivals ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanabata Festival|1=UNKNOWN}}) * First Saturday of August — {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Grand Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} Eve Festival, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nashi Fireworks|1=UNKNOWN}} Safety Prayer, {{ill|lt=Parishioners|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} Officers’ Report Ceremony, Parishioner Safety Prayer * First Sunday of August — Grand Festival Main Ceremony, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hama-yumi|1=UNKNOWN}} Ritual, {{ill|lt=Devotees|Q1579384|WD=Q1579384|1=Veneration}} Report Ceremony, Devotee Safety Prayer * {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856|1=September 9}} — Five Seasonal Festivals ({{ill|lt=Chrysanthemum Festival|Q16909838|WD=Q16909838|1=Chrysanthemum Day}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōyō no Sekku|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Autumnal Equinox Day — Autumn Joint Ancestral Festival (Soreisha) * {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}} — {{ill|lt=Niinamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} * {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}} — {{ill|lt=Great Purification|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} Rite, {{ill|lt=New Year’s Eve|Q7245702|WD=Q7245702|1=Chinese New Year's Eve}} Festival ; Monthly * 1st — Monthly Festival * 15th — Monthly Festival ; Daily * Daily Offering Rite === Other === * One of the shrine’s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gonnegi|1=UNKNOWN}} (assistant priests), who is also a motorcyclist, began providing support services for riders. This led to the shrine becoming known as a “Motorcycle Shrine.”[https://hamamatsu-lab.jp/goout/2327.html Published: 2021/10/04 “Ōtoshi Shrine, the Motorcycle Shrine of Hamamatsu, the Home of Motorcycles” Hamalabo – Hamamatsu Research Institute][https://forride.jp/motorcycle/sizuoka-touring Published: 2020/08/23 Updated: 2020/08/23 “A Local Motorcycle Girl’s Guide! Shizuoka Holiday Touring Spots” PLAY For Ride] === References === * “Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals Nationwide,” Association of Shinto Shrines, 1995 === Citations === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.ootoshi-jinja.or.jp/ Engishiki Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine] {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo Ward, Hamamatsu City]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:オートバイ]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Torii gate and worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|44|14.2|N|137|46|15.9|E}}}} |Location= {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}} 1484-1 Tennō-chō |Coordinates= {{coord|34|44|14.2|N|137|46|15.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities= {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} {{ill|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} (associated) {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}} {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta Taneko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Emperor Chōkei|Q464039|WD=Q464039|1=Emperor Chōkei}} {{ill|lt=Emperor Kameyama|Q360972|WD=Q360972|1=Emperor Kameyama}} {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Uda|Q440538|WD=Q440538|1=Emperor Go-Uda}} {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nijō|Q374075|WD=Q374075|1=Emperor Go-Nijō}} {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}} {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Murakami|Q440447|WD=Q440447|1=Emperor Go-Murakami}} |Shrine rank= Former village shrine; Shikinaisha |Established= Unknown |Annual festival= Nearest Sunday to {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} Grand Festival |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ōtoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine''' (てんのうぐうおおとしじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Tennō-chō, {{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. One of its component shrines, Ōtoshi Shrine, is listed among the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province|Q11642130|WD=Q11642130|lt=Engishiki shrines|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}}. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. Originally, the shrine consisted of three separate shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tennōgū|1=UNKNOWN}}, Tennō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|Q11436758|WD=Q11436758|1=Ōtoshi Shrines}}—which were later merged. Subsequently, the tutelary deities of four parish towns were also enshrined together, resulting in the present form. The collective name Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine came into use thereafter. === Enshrined deities === ''' Tennōgū ''' * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta Taneko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ''' Tennō Shrine ''' * {{ill|lt=Emperor Chōkei|Q464039|WD=Q464039|1=Emperor Chōkei}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Kameyama|Q360972|WD=Q360972|1=Emperor Kameyama}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Uda|Q440538|WD=Q440538|1=Emperor Go-Uda}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nijō|Q374075|WD=Q374075|1=Emperor Go-Nijō}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Murakami|Q440447|WD=Q440447|1=Emperor Go-Murakami}} ''' Ōtoshi Shrine ''' * Ōtoshi-no-Kami === Subsidiary shrines === * Mikuwainari Shrine * Soreisha (Ancestral Spirit Shrine) === Events === * {{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}} — New Year’s Day Festival, Burnt Offerings Festival, {{ill|lt=Genshisai|Q11387821|WD=Q11387821|1=Genshi-sai}} * {{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228|1=January 7}} — {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Five Seasonal Festivals|1=UNKNOWN}} (Seven Herbs Festival / Wakakusa no Sekku) * {{ill|lt=January 28|Q2285|WD=Q2285|1=January 28}} — Burnt Offerings Festival * {{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|WD=Q2315|1=February 3}} — {{ill|lt=Setsubun|Q746798|WD=Q746798|1=Setsubun}} Festival * First Day of the Horse — {{ill|lt=Hatsuuma|Q510847|WD=Q510847|1=Hatsuuma}} Festival * {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}} — {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} / {{ill|lt=National Foundation Day|Q11604982|WD=Q11604982|1=Kigensetsu}} Festival * {{ill|lt=March 3|Q2391|WD=Q2391|1=March 3}} — Five Seasonal Festivals ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Peach Festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Jōshi no Sekku|1=UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141|WD=Q1049141|1=Vernal Equinox Day}} — Spring Joint Ancestral Festival (Soreisha) * First Day of the Horse — Mikuwainari Shrine Annual Festival * {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} — Five Seasonal Festivals ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iris Festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tango no Sekku|1=UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill|lt=May 15|Q2562|WD=Q2562|1=May 15}} — {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tea Offering|1=UNKNOWN}} Ceremony * {{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}} — {{ill|lt=Great Purification|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} Rite of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nagoshi|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=July 7|Q2694|WD=Q2694|1=July 7}} — Five Seasonal Festivals ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanabata Festival|1=UNKNOWN}}) * First Saturday of August — {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Grand Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} Eve Festival, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nashi Fireworks|1=UNKNOWN}} Safety Prayer, {{ill|lt=Parishioners|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} Officers’ Report Ceremony, Parishioner Safety Prayer * First Sunday of August — Grand Festival Main Ceremony, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hama-yumi|1=UNKNOWN}} Ritual, {{ill|lt=Devotees|Q1579384|WD=Q1579384|1=Veneration}} Report Ceremony, Devotee Safety Prayer * {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856|1=September 9}} — Five Seasonal Festivals ({{ill|lt=Chrysanthemum Festival|Q16909838|WD=Q16909838|1=Chrysanthemum Day}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōyō no Sekku|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Autumnal Equinox Day — Autumn Joint Ancestral Festival (Soreisha) * {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}} — {{ill|lt=Niinamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} * {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}} — {{ill|lt=Great Purification|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} Rite, {{ill|lt=New Year’s Eve|Q7245702|WD=Q7245702|1=Chinese New Year's Eve}} Festival ; Monthly * 1st — Monthly Festival * 15th — Monthly Festival ; Daily * Daily Offering Rite === Other === * One of the shrine’s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gonnegi|1=UNKNOWN}} (assistant priests), who is also a motorcyclist, began providing support services for riders. This led to the shrine becoming known as a “Motorcycle Shrine.”[https://hamamatsu-lab.jp/goout/2327.html Published: 2021/10/04 “Ōtoshi Shrine, the Motorcycle Shrine of Hamamatsu, the Home of Motorcycles” Hamalabo – Hamamatsu Research Institute][https://forride.jp/motorcycle/sizuoka-touring Published: 2020/08/23 Updated: 2020/08/23 “A Local Motorcycle Girl’s Guide! Shizuoka Holiday Touring Spots” PLAY For Ride] === References === * “Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals Nationwide,” Association of Shinto Shrines, 1995 === Citations === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.ootoshi-jinja.or.jp/ Engishiki Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine] {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo Ward, Hamamatsu City]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:オートバイ]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:moved from a redlink]]

Shikinaisha |Established= Unknown |Annual festival= Nearest Sunday to {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} Grand Festival |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ōtoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine''' (てんのうぐうおおとしじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Tennō-chō, {{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. One of its component shrines, Ōtoshi Shrine, is listed among the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province|Q11642130|WD=Q11642130|lt=Engishiki shrines|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}}. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. Originally, the shrine consisted of three separate shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tennōgū|1=UNKNOWN}}, Tennō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|Q11436758|WD=Q11436758|1=Ōtoshi Shrines}}—which were later merged. Subsequently, the tutelary deities of four parish towns were also enshrined together, resulting in the present form. The collective name Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine came into use thereafter. === Enshrined deities === ''' Tennōgū ''' * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, Monthly Festival ; Daily * Daily Offering Rite === Other === * One of the shrine’s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gonnegi|1=UNKNOWN}} (assistant priests), who is also a motorcyclist, began providing support services for riders. This led to the shrine becoming known as a “Motorcycle Shrine.”[https://hamamatsu-lab.jp/goout/2327.html Published: 2021/10/04 “Ōtoshi Shrine, the Motorcycle Shrine of Hamamatsu, the Home of Motorcycles” Hamalabo – Hamamatsu Research Institute][https://forride.jp/motorcycle/sizuoka-touring Published: 2020/08/23 Updated: 2020/08/23 “A Local Motorcycle Girl’s Guide! Shikinaisha |Established= Unknown |Annual festival= Nearest Sunday to {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} Grand Festival |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ōtoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine''' (てんのうぐうおおとしじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Tennō-chō, {{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. One of its component shrines, Ōtoshi Shrine, is listed among the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province|Q11642130|WD=Q11642130|lt=Engishiki shrines|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}}. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. Originally, the shrine consisted of three separate shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tennōgū|1=UNKNOWN}}, Tennō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|Q11436758|WD=Q11436758|1=Ōtoshi Shrines}}—which were later merged. Subsequently, the tutelary deities of four parish towns were also enshrined together, resulting in the present form. The collective name Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine came into use thereafter. === Enshrined deities === ''' Tennōgū ''' * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta Taneko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ''' Tennō Shrine ''' * {{ill|lt=Emperor Chōkei|Q464039|WD=Q464039|1=Emperor Chōkei}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Kameyama|Q360972|WD=Q360972|1=Emperor Kameyama}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Uda|Q440538|WD=Q440538|1=Emperor Go-Uda}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nijō|Q374075|WD=Q374075|1=Emperor Go-Nijō}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Murakami|Q440447|WD=Q440447|1=Emperor Go-Murakami}} ''' Ōtoshi Shrine ''' * Ōtoshi-no-Kami === Subsidiary shrines === * Mikuwainari Shrine * Soreisha (Ancestral Spirit Shrine) === Events === * {{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}} — New Year’s Day {{ill|lt=Great Purification|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} Rite, {{ill|lt=New Year’s Eve|Q7245702|WD=Q7245702|1=Chinese New Year's Eve}} Festival ; Monthly * 1st — Monthly Festival * 15th — Monthly Festival ; Daily * Daily Offering Rite === Other === * One of the shrine’s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gonnegi|1=UNKNOWN}} (assistant priests), who is also a motorcyclist, began providing support services for riders. This led to the shrine becoming known as a “Motorcycle Shrine.”[https://hamamatsu-lab.jp/goout/2327.html Published: 2021/10/04 “Ōtoshi Shrine, the Motorcycle Shrine of Hamamatsu, the Home of Motorcycles” Hamalabo – Hamamatsu Research Institute][https://forride.jp/motorcycle/sizuoka-touring Published: 2020/08/23 Updated: 2020/08/23 “A Local Motorcycle Girl’s Guide! {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|44|14.2|N|137|46|15.9|E}}}} |Location= {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}} 1484-1 Tennō-chō |Coordinates= {{coord|34|44|14.2|N|137|46|15.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities= {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} {{ill|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} (associated) {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}} {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta Taneko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Emperor Chōkei|Q464039|WD=Q464039|1=Emperor Chōkei}} {{ill|lt=Emperor Kameyama|Q360972|WD=Q360972|1=Emperor Kameyama}} {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Uda|Q440538|WD=Q440538|1=Emperor Go-Uda}} {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nijō|Q374075|WD=Q374075|1=Emperor Go-Nijō}} {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}} {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Murakami|Q440447|WD=Q440447|1=Emperor Go-Murakami}} |Shrine rank= Former village shrine; Shikinaisha |Established= Unknown |Annual festival= Nearest Sunday to {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} Grand Festival |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ōtoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine''' (てんのうぐうおおとしじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Tennō-chō, {{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. One of its component shrines, Ōtoshi Shrine, is listed among the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province|Q11642130|WD=Q11642130|lt=Engishiki shrines|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}}. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. Originally, the shrine consisted of three separate shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tennōgū|1=UNKNOWN}}, Tennō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|Q11436758|WD=Q11436758|1=Ōtoshi Shrines}}—which were later merged. Subsequently, the tutelary deities of four parish towns were also enshrined together, resulting in the present form. The collective name Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine came into use thereafter. === Enshrined deities === ''' Tennōgū ''' * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta Taneko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ''' Tennō Shrine ''' * {{ill|lt=Emperor Chōkei|Q464039|WD=Q464039|1=Emperor Chōkei}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Kameyama|Q360972|WD=Q360972|1=Emperor Kameyama}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Uda|Q440538|WD=Q440538|1=Emperor Go-Uda}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nijō|Q374075|WD=Q374075|1=Emperor Go-Nijō}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Murakami|Q440447|WD=Q440447|1=Emperor Go-Murakami}} ''' Ōtoshi Shrine ''' * Ōtoshi-no-Kami === Subsidiary shrines === * Mikuwainari Shrine * Soreisha (Ancestral Spirit Shrine) === Events === * {{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}} — New Year’s Day Festival, Burnt Offerings Festival, {{ill|lt=Genshisai|Q11387821|WD=Q11387821|1=Genshi-sai}} * {{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228|1=January 7}} — {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Five Seasonal Festivals|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Great Purification|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} Rite, {{ill|lt=New Year’s Eve|Q7245702|WD=Q7245702|1=Chinese New Year's Eve}} Festival ; Monthly * 1st — Monthly Festival * 15th — Monthly Festival ; Daily * Daily Offering Rite === Other === * One of the shrine’s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gonnegi|1=UNKNOWN}} (assistant priests), who is also a motorcyclist, began providing support services for riders. This led to the shrine becoming known as a “Motorcycle Shrine.”[https://hamamatsu-lab.jp/goout/2327.html Published: 2021/10/04 “Ōtoshi Shrine, the Motorcycle Shrine of Hamamatsu, the Home of Motorcycles” Hamalabo – Hamamatsu Research Institute][https://forride.jp/motorcycle/sizuoka-touring Published: 2020/08/23 Updated: 2020/08/23 “A Local Motorcycle Girl’s Guide! Shikinaisha |Established= Unknown |Annual festival= Nearest Sunday to {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} Grand Festival |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ōtoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine''' (てんのうぐうおおとしじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Tennō-chō, {{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. One of its component shrines, Ōtoshi Shrine, is listed among the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province|Q11642130|WD=Q11642130|lt=Engishiki shrines|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}}. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. Originally, the shrine consisted of three separate shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tennōgū|1=UNKNOWN}}, Tennō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|Q11436758|WD=Q11436758|1=Ōtoshi Shrines}}—which were later merged. Subsequently, the tutelary deities of four parish towns were also enshrined together, resulting in the present form. The collective name Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine came into use thereafter. === Enshrined deities === ''' Tennōgū ''' * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hama-yumi|1=UNKNOWN}} Ritual, {{ill|lt=Devotees|Q1579384|WD=Q1579384|1=Veneration}} Report Ceremony, Devotee Safety Prayer * {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856|1=September 9}} — Five Seasonal Festivals ({{ill|lt=Chrysanthemum Festival|Q16909838|WD=Q16909838|1=Chrysanthemum Day}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōyō no Sekku|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Autumnal Equinox Day — Autumn Joint Ancestral Festival (Soreisha) * {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}} — {{ill|lt=Niinamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} * {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}} — {{ill|lt=Great Purification|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} Rite, {{ill|lt=New Year’s Eve|Q7245702|WD=Q7245702|1=Chinese New Year's Eve}} Festival ; Monthly * 1st — Monthly Festival * 15th — Monthly Festival ; Daily * Daily Offering Rite === Other === * One of the shrine’s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gonnegi|1=UNKNOWN}} (assistant priests), who is also a motorcyclist, began providing support services for riders. This led to the shrine becoming known as a “Motorcycle Shrine.”[https://hamamatsu-lab.jp/goout/2327.html Published: 2021/10/04 “Ōtoshi Shrine, the Motorcycle Shrine of Hamamatsu, the Home of Motorcycles” Hamalabo – Hamamatsu Research Institute][https://forride.jp/motorcycle/sizuoka-touring Published: 2020/08/23 Updated: 2020/08/23 “A Local Motorcycle Girl’s Guide! Shizuoka Holiday Touring Spots” PLAY For Ride] === References === * “Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals Nationwide,” Association of Shinto Shrines, 1995 === Citations === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.ootoshi-jinja.or.jp/ Engishiki Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine] {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo Ward, Hamamatsu City]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:オートバイ]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

Shikinaisha |Established= Unknown |Annual festival= Nearest Sunday to {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} Grand Festival |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ōtoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine''' (てんのうぐうおおとしじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Tennō-chō, {{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. One of its component shrines, Ōtoshi Shrine, is listed among the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province|Q11642130|WD=Q11642130|lt=Engishiki shrines|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}}. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. Originally, the shrine consisted of three separate shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tennōgū|1=UNKNOWN}}, Tennō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|Q11436758|WD=Q11436758|1=Ōtoshi Shrines}}—which were later merged. Subsequently, the tutelary deities of four parish towns were also enshrined together, resulting in the present form. The collective name Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine came into use thereafter. === Enshrined deities === ''' Tennōgū ''' * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta Taneko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ''' Tennō Shrine ''' * {{ill|lt=Emperor Chōkei|Q464039|WD=Q464039|1=Emperor Chōkei}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Kameyama|Q360972|WD=Q360972|1=Emperor Kameyama}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Uda|Q440538|WD=Q440538|1=Emperor Go-Uda}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nijō|Q374075|WD=Q374075|1=Emperor Go-Nijō}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Murakami|Q440447|WD=Q440447|1=Emperor Go-Murakami}} ''' Ōtoshi Shrine ''' * Ōtoshi-no-Kami === Subsidiary shrines === * Mikuwainari Shrine * Soreisha (Ancestral Spirit Shrine) === Events === * {{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}} — New Year’s Day Festival, Burnt Offerings Festival, {{ill|lt=Genshisai|Q11387821|WD=Q11387821|1=Genshi-sai}} * {{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228|1=January 7}} — {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Five Seasonal Festivals|1=UNKNOWN}} (Seven Herbs Festival / Wakakusa no Sekku) * {{ill|lt=January 28|Q2285|WD=Q2285|1=January 28}} — Burnt Offerings Festival * {{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|WD=Q2315|1=February 3}} — {{ill|lt=Setsubun|Q746798|WD=Q746798|1=Setsubun}} Festival * First Day of the Horse — {{ill|lt=Hatsuuma|Q510847|WD=Q510847|1=Hatsuuma}} Festival * {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}} — {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} / {{ill|lt=National Foundation Day|Q11604982|WD=Q11604982|1=Kigensetsu}} Festival * {{ill|lt=March 3|Q2391|WD=Q2391|1=March 3}} — Five Seasonal Festivals ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Peach Festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Jōshi no Sekku|1=UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141|WD=Q1049141|1=Vernal Equinox Day}} — Spring Joint Ancestral Festival (Soreisha) * First Day of the Horse — Mikuwainari Shrine Annual Festival * {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} — Five Seasonal Festivals ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iris Festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tango no Sekku|1=UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill|lt=May 15|Q2562|WD=Q2562|1=May 15}} — {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tea Offering|1=UNKNOWN}} Ceremony * {{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}} — {{ill|lt=Great Purification|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} Rite of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nagoshi|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=July 7|Q2694|WD=Q2694|1=July 7}} — Five Seasonal Festivals ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanabata Festival|1=UNKNOWN}}) * First Saturday of August — {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Grand Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} Eve Festival, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nashi Fireworks|1=UNKNOWN}} Safety Prayer, {{ill|lt=Parishioners|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} Officers’ Report Ceremony, Parishioner Safety Prayer * First Sunday of August — Grand Festival Main Ceremony, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hama-yumi|1=UNKNOWN}} Ritual, {{ill|lt=Devotees|Q1579384|WD=Q1579384|1=Veneration}} Report Ceremony, Devotee Safety Prayer * {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856|1=September 9}} — Five Seasonal Festivals ({{ill|lt=Chrysanthemum Festival|Q16909838|WD=Q16909838|1=Chrysanthemum Day}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōyō no Sekku|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Autumnal Equinox Day — Autumn Joint Ancestral Festival (Soreisha) * {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}} — {{ill|lt=Niinamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} * {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}} — {{ill|lt=Great Purification|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} Rite, {{ill|lt=New Year’s Eve|Q7245702|WD=Q7245702|1=Chinese New Year's Eve}} Festival ; Monthly * 1st — Monthly Festival * 15th — Monthly Festival ; Daily * Daily Offering Rite === Other === * One of the shrine’s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gonnegi|1=UNKNOWN}} (assistant priests), who is also a motorcyclist, began providing support services for riders. This led to the shrine becoming known as a “Motorcycle Shrine.”[https://hamamatsu-lab.jp/goout/2327.html Published: 2021/10/04 “Ōtoshi Shrine, the Motorcycle Shrine of Hamamatsu, the Home of Motorcycles” Hamalabo – Hamamatsu Research Institute][https://forride.jp/motorcycle/sizuoka-touring Published: 2020/08/23 Updated: 2020/08/23 “A Local Motorcycle Girl’s Guide! Shizuoka Holiday Touring Spots” PLAY For Ride] === References === * “Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals Nationwide,” Association of Shinto Shrines, 1995 === Citations === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.ootoshi-jinja.or.jp/ Engishiki Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine] {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo Ward, Hamamatsu City]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:オートバイ]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

Shikinaisha |Established= Unknown |Annual festival= Nearest Sunday to {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} Grand Festival |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ōtoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine''' (てんのうぐうおおとしじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Tennō-chō, {{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. One of its component shrines, Ōtoshi Shrine, is listed among the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province|Q11642130|WD=Q11642130|lt=Engishiki shrines|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}}. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. Originally, the shrine consisted of three separate shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tennōgū|1=UNKNOWN}}, Tennō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|Q11436758|WD=Q11436758|1=Ōtoshi Shrines}}—which were later merged. Subsequently, the tutelary deities of four parish towns were also enshrined together, resulting in the present form. The collective name Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine came into use thereafter. === Enshrined deities === ''' Tennōgū ''' * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta Taneko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ''' Tennō Shrine ''' * {{ill|lt=Emperor Chōkei|Q464039|WD=Q464039|1=Emperor Chōkei}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Kameyama|Q360972|WD=Q360972|1=Emperor Kameyama}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Uda|Q440538|WD=Q440538|1=Emperor Go-Uda}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nijō|Q374075|WD=Q374075|1=Emperor Go-Nijō}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Murakami|Q440447|WD=Q440447|1=Emperor Go-Murakami}} ''' Ōtoshi Shrine ''' * Ōtoshi-no-Kami === Subsidiary shrines === * Mikuwainari Shrine * Soreisha (Ancestral Spirit Shrine) === Events === * {{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}} — New Year’s Day Festival, Burnt Offerings Festival, {{ill|lt=Genshisai|Q11387821|WD=Q11387821|1=Genshi-sai}} * {{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228|1=January 7}} — {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Five Seasonal Festivals|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Great Purification|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} Rite, {{ill|lt=New Year’s Eve|Q7245702|WD=Q7245702|1=Chinese New Year's Eve}} Festival ; Monthly * 1st — Monthly Festival * 15th — Monthly Festival ; Daily * Daily Offering Rite === Other === * One of the shrine’s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gonnegi|1=UNKNOWN}} (assistant priests), who is also a motorcyclist, began providing support services for riders. This led to the shrine becoming known as a “Motorcycle Shrine.”[https://hamamatsu-lab.jp/goout/2327.html Published: 2021/10/04 “Ōtoshi Shrine, the Motorcycle Shrine of Hamamatsu, the Home of Motorcycles” Hamalabo – Hamamatsu Research Institute][https://forride.jp/motorcycle/sizuoka-touring Published: 2020/08/23 Updated: 2020/08/23 “A Local Motorcycle Girl’s Guide! Shikinaisha |Established= Unknown |Annual festival= Nearest Sunday to {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} Grand Festival |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ōtoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine''' (てんのうぐうおおとしじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Tennō-chō, {{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. One of its component shrines, Ōtoshi Shrine, is listed among the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province|Q11642130|WD=Q11642130|lt=Engishiki shrines|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}}. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. Originally, the shrine consisted of three separate shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tennōgū|1=UNKNOWN}}, Tennō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|Q11436758|WD=Q11436758|1=Ōtoshi Shrines}}—which were later merged. Subsequently, the tutelary deities of four parish towns were also enshrined together, resulting in the present form. The collective name Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine came into use thereafter. === Enshrined deities === ''' Tennōgū ''' * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, Monthly Festival ; Daily * Daily Offering Rite === Other === * One of the shrine’s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gonnegi|1=UNKNOWN}} (assistant priests), who is also a motorcyclist, began providing support services for riders. This led to the shrine becoming known as a “Motorcycle Shrine.”[https://hamamatsu-lab.jp/goout/2327.html Published: 2021/10/04 “Ōtoshi Shrine, the Motorcycle Shrine of Hamamatsu, the Home of Motorcycles” Hamalabo – Hamamatsu Research Institute][https://forride.jp/motorcycle/sizuoka-touring Published: 2020/08/23 Updated: 2020/08/23 “A Local Motorcycle Girl’s Guide! Shizuoka Holiday Touring Spots” PLAY For Ride] === References === * “Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals Nationwide,” Association of Shinto Shrines, 1995 === Citations === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.ootoshi-jinja.or.jp/ Engishiki Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine] {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine]][[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chuo Ward, Hamamatsu City]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:Motorcycles]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

Shikinaisha |Established= Unknown |Annual festival= Nearest Sunday to {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}} Grand Festival |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ōtoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine''' (てんのうぐうおおとしじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Tennō-chō, {{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. One of its component shrines, Ōtoshi Shrine, is listed among the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province|Q11642130|WD=Q11642130|lt=Engishiki shrines|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}}. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. Originally, the shrine consisted of three separate shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tennōgū|1=UNKNOWN}}, Tennō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|Q11436758|WD=Q11436758|1=Ōtoshi Shrines}}—which were later merged. Subsequently, the tutelary deities of four parish towns were also enshrined together, resulting in the present form. The collective name Tennōgū Ōtoshi Shrine came into use thereafter. === Enshrined deities === ''' Tennōgū ''' * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}},
